commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a TUI surrendered-run-id set so late chat delta/final/error events are skipped after a matching `sessions.changed` history reload.

PR surface: Source +99. Total +99 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reloads history on matching `sessions.changed`, and history replay can rebuild assistant rows without run IDs. I did not run a live Telegram/TUI reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a code fix and contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge; proof is a human-only gate, so this should not be routed as an automatic repair marker yet.

target.title: fix(tui): suppress duplicate assistant message after sessions.changed event
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes a TUI rendering bug where assistant messages appear twice when a `sessions.changed` event with `reason: "new"` fires while a chat run is still streaming. This commonly occurs when using the Telegram plugin â€” the assistant's reply renders once via the chat event pipeline, then a second time via the history replay triggered by `sessions.changed`.

## Why This Change Was Made

**Root cause: race condition between `handleChatEvent` final and `handleSessionsChangedEvent` history reload.**

When a `sessions.changed` event with `reason: "new"` fires while a chat run is still streaming:

1. `handleSessionsChangedEvent` calls `clearTrackedRunState()` which clears all tracking state including `finalizedRuns`, `sessionRuns`, and the stream assembler
2. `loadHistory()` is called, which runs `chatLog.clearAll()` â€” removing the streaming assistant component â€” then replays the message from server history as a static entry
3. When the `chat` final event arrives afterward, both `finalizedRuns` and `streamingRuns` are empty, so `chatLog.finalizeAssistant()` cannot find the existing component and **appends a duplicate**

**Fix:** Introduce a `surrenderedToHistoryRunIds` set that collects all tracked runIds **before** `clearTrackedRunState()` in `handleSessionsChangedEvent`. In `handleChatEvent`, check this set and skip late-arriving delta/final events whose runId was already handed off to history replay. A 15-second cleanup timer prevents the set from growing unbounded.

## User Impact

- TUI no longer shows duplicate assistant messages when receiving messages via external channels (Telegram, etc.)
- No changes to config, Gateway protocol, or plugin behavior
- Activity indicator and session state cleanup still work correctly for surrendered runs

## What Changed

1 file, +99 lines:

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts` | Add `surrenderedToHistoryRunIds` set, populate before `clearTrackedRunState()`, check in `handleChatEvent` to skip duplicates |

## Evidence

- Branch base: `origin/main` (9a735bea03)
- Environment: Linux x64, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.8

### Test results

```text
# TUI event handlers â€” 79/79 passed
pnpm test src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts
 âś“ 79 passed

# TUI session actions â€” 34/34 passed
pnpm test src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts
 âś“ 34 passed

# Chat log component â€” 19/19 passed
pnpm test src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts
 âś“ 19 passed
```

### Reproduction scenario

1. Open `openclaw-tui` connected to gateway with Telegram plugin enabled
2. Send a message via Telegram to the bot
3. Before fix: assistant reply rendered twice in TUI
4. After fix: assistant reply rendered once

## Root Cause Detail

- `handleSessionsChangedEvent` ([tui-event-handlers.ts:844](src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts#L844)) clears ALL tracking state via `clearTrackedRunState()`
- `loadHistory()` ([tui-session-actions.ts:472](src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts#L472)) calls `chatLog.clearAll()` removing streaming components
- History replay ([tui-session-actions.ts:503](src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts#L503)) calls `finalizeAssistant(text)` without runId â€” creates a new static component
- Late-arriving final event ([chat-log.ts:313](src/tui/components/chat-log.ts#L313)) can't find the streaming component â†’ appends a duplicate

đź¤– Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
